content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach demonstrates specific, timely reads throughout. Turn 2 catches the linguistic caveat ('I think') immediately and names it operatively. Turn 4 reads the body explicitly. Turn 6 names the safety/distance state before asking the next question. Turn 10 reads the pattern (scanning-for-safety) and reflects it back. Critically, when the somatic shutdown begins in turn 11, the coach immediately pivots in turn 12 to track the cold somatically ('where is it?') rather than continuing the trauma inquiry, and in turn 14 reads the dissociation signal and offers grounding (chair underneath you) — a pattern-level read of the freeze/floating signature met with somatic re-grounding. Reads consistently precede questions.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Catches the linguistic caveat 'I think' immediately and names it — classic AoA read of the said-vs-shown."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Explicit somatic check-in: 'what does it feel like in your body right now.'"},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the current state ('some safety, some distance') before inquiring — read precedes question."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Pattern-level read of the scanning/hypervigilance system, named operatively."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the somatic shutdown the moment it appears; tracks the cold specifically rather than staying in content."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Reads the dissociation/floating and pivots to grounding (chair, body) — pattern read of the freeze signature."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature"],
  "notes": "The rush signature wasn't strongly active here, so its absence isn't a real miss. Reads were specific (linguistic, somatic, pattern-level) and consistently timely."
}
